tests.name: ANEMIA PROFILE
tests.specimenPreparation: Gently mix the lavender tubes 4-5 times after collection. Allow the SST tubes to clot for 30 minutes. Spin the SST tubes for 3500RPM for 10 Minutues
tests.storageTransport: store and transport at room temperature.
tests.rejectionCriteria: samples received after stability. Unlabelled or mismatched specimens. Samples in expired containers.

tests.name: CBC WITH DIFFERENTIAL
tests.specimenPreparation: Gently mix the lavender tubes 4-5 times after collection

tests.name: HEMOGLOBIN A1C
tests.specimenPreparation: Gently mix the lavender tubes 4-5 times after collection

tests.name: APTT
tests.specimenPreparation: gently mix the tube 4-5 times after collections. Send to lab immediately. If more than 6 hours delay is expected, soin the tubes at 3500RPM for 10 minutes. Sepearte the plasma in a labelled secondary tube, freeze the specimen. Send frozen specimen to lab

tests.name: GASTRO INFECTION PANEL
tests.patientPreparation: instruct the patient to collect stool specimen in a sterile container
tests.specimenPreparation: swirl the fecal swab in the sttol container. Remove access stool. Transfer the swab in the tube with liquid. Send the fecal swab in the tube to the lab.
tests.storageTransport: store and transport at Room temperature for 48 hours. Refrigerate if delays are expected
tests.rejectionCriteria: frozen specimen. Specimen received after stability period.

tests.name: H. PYLORI ANTIGEN, STOOL
tests.patientPreparation: To avoid false negative results, no antibiotics, proton pump inhibitors, or a bismuth preparation should be administered to patient for 14 days prior to specimen collection.
tests.specimenPreparation: Transfer stool to an unpreserved stool transport vial.
tests.storageTransport: Refrigerated: 72 hours; Frozen: indefinite
tests.rejectionCriteria: Gastric specimens, swabs or tissue. Specimens in media or preservatives. Watery or diarrheal stool.

tests.name: H.pylori Breath test
tests.patientPreparation:
Patient should fast at least one hour before collection of baseline breath sample. Refer to the current Patient Services Collection of Urea Breath tests for Helicobacter Pylori detection using PyloPlus UBT collection devices
 Collect the BASELINE breath sample using the BLUE breath collection bag.
Replace the cap firmly on the mouthpiece of the bag.
 Collect the POSTSAMPLE breath sample using the PINK breath collection bag fifteen (15) minutes after drinking the 13C-ureasolution. Replace the cap firmly on the mouthpiece of the bag.
Special Collection Procedures:
 Advise the patient NOT to rinse the inside of his/her mouth with the 13C-urea solution before swallowing.
 If the patient has not held their breath for 10-seconds or does not fill the bag completely, there is a possibility of a test result will not be obtainable.
 Not fully closing the bag may cause the breath sample to slowly leak out.
 If patient fails to fill the bag completely, air can be expelled, and the patient can try again.
tests.storageTransport: store and transport at room temperature
tests.rejectionCriteria:
refrigerated or frozen samples will be rejected.Insufficient breath for analysis
 Single collection bags
 Specimens from patients<3 years old
 Specimens not submitted in PyloPlus UBT Collection bags.
 Uncapped bags

tests.name: THROAT CULTURE
tests.patientPreparation:
Obtain specimen from the Pharynx. Specimen must be obtained before antimicrobial therapy has been administered. Specimen source required
a) Place gentle pressure on the tongue with a tongue depress or.
b) Extend the swab between the tonsillar pillars and behind the uvula, avoiding the tongue, buccal mucosa,
and uvula.
c) Sweep the swab back and forth across the posterior pharynx, tonsillar are as, and any inflamed or exudative
are as. Place swab in transport. 
NOTE: Do not obtain specimen if the epiglottis is inflamed, since sampling may trigger life threatening respiratory obstruction.
tests.storageTransport: Refrigerated
tests.rejectionCriteria:
a) Swab Submitted in viral transport medium
b) Swab submitted in expired transport medium
c) Swab with cotton fiber or sponge.
d) Swab on wooden shaft
e) Refrigerated Swab received after 48 hours of collection time
f) Room temperature Swab received after 2hours of collection time
g) Unlabeled specimen or name discrepancy between specimen and request label

tests.name: STI PANEL
tests.patientPreparation: collect mid-steam urine specimen in sterile container. aliquot immediately in the yellow aptima urine tube
tests.storageTransport: Room temperature for 24 hrs. refrigerate after 24 hours

tests.name: CDS/CONF SALIVA/ ORAL SWAB
tests.specimenTube: QUANTISAL ORAL FLUID COLLECTION DEVICE FOR ORAL DRUG TESTS
tests.specimenPreparation: Quantisal collection device consist of a pad, which absorbs the saliva, and then the whole collector is placed into a stabilization buffer for transportation to a laboratory for processing. See Colection Instruction.
tests.storageTransport: Room Temperature
tests.rejectionCriteria:
Specimens submitted in any collection device other than Quantisal oral fluid device
 Specimens received with any food debris or foreign objects present in the sample.
 Multiple swabs present in a single device or No swab present with the device
 If Collection device expired
 Quantity not sufficient for analysis. No identification

tests.name: Biopsy
tests.minimumVolume: use 10x the volume of formalin relative to the specimen size.
tests.specimenPreparation:
Always follow standard precautions when handling biological materials.
Use appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) at all times.
Specimen Preservation
Preserve all tissue specimens in 10% buffered formalin to prevent cellular degeneration.
Use 34 times the tissue volume in formalin, with a minimum of 30 mL.
Ensure the tissue can float freely in the container without being compressed.
Container Requirements
Place each specimen in a properly sized container with enough formalin to fully submerge the tissue.
A general guideline: 
If submitting multiple specimens, use separate containers for each.
Seal all containers securely and check for leakage before transport.
Requisition Form Requirements
A completed pathology requisition form must accompany every specimen.
The form must clearly and accurately identify:
What the specimen is (e.g., mass, tumor, bone).
Where it was obtained (anatomical site).
Laterality (e.g., RIGHT or LEFT side).
Ensure that patient and specimen identification match exactly between the container label and the requisition form.
tests.storageTransport: Room Temperature
tests.rejectionCriteria:
Specimens may be rejected or delayed in processing if any of the following conditions are present:
Improper labeling: Specimen containers must include accurate patient identification and specimen details. Unlabeled or mislabeled samples will not be processed.
Missing clinical history: Relevant clinical information must be provided on the requisition form to ensure appropriate diagnostic evaluation.
Frozen or unfixed tissue

tests.name: URINALYSIS C/K CULTURE
subTests: Urinalysis if abnormal reflex to culture
tests.patientPreparation: MID STREAM CLEAN CATCH URINE SPECIMEN
tests.specimenPreparation: transfer the urine specimen into grey top culture and sensitivity tube immediately after collection. Send the sterile cup and grey top tube to the lab
tests.storageTransport: Refrigerated: 48 hours
tests.rejectionCriteria: FROZEN IS REJECTED. Quantity not sufficient for analysis; tubes with boric acid or tartaric acid.
tests.specialNotes: if urinalysis is abnormal then the test will be reflexed to urine culture
